name: OSMnx (Street Networks from OpenStreetMap)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Geoff Boeing / USC; uses free Overpass API

auth.notes: Uses public Overpass API (OpenStreetMap); no auth needed

performance.notes: Overpass API download speed varies; large cities take 30s+ for full network

agent_readiness.notes: Download and analyze street networks from OpenStreetMap. G = ox.graph_from_place('Manhattan, NY'). ox.plot_graph(G). Routing: ox.shortest_path(G, orig, dest). Stats: ox.basic_stats(G). Building footprints, parks, POIs. Elevation data. Isochrones. GeoPandas integration. NetworkX graph representation. Used in urban planning, routing research, transportation analysis. The go-to tool for street network analysis in Python. By Geoff Boeing (USC).

gotchas: Overpass API rate limiting for large queries
Graph quality depends on OSM data completeness in region
Large cities (NYC, London) take significant download time
Projection handling requires careful CRS management
best_when: Academic research needing free street network data
avoid_when: Production routing apps (use HERE, Google, Mapbox instead)
Real-time routing (Overpass not a live routing API)
summary: OSMnx downloads and analyzes street networks from OpenStreetMap. graph_from_place('City'), graph_from_point(lat, lon). NetworkX graph with routing, basic_stats, isochrones. Building footprints, POIs. GeoPandas integration. The standard tool for academic street network analysis. Uses free Overpass API. For production routing: use commercial APIs (HERE, Google, Mapbox).
